content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Candace Dauphinot 
Aptos Incorporation Group 
P.O. Box 485 
Aptos, CA 
95001-0485 
Dear Ms. Dauphinot: 
September 11, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal 
Assistance 
Our File No. I-89-451 
You have requested information concerning any recent changes 
in the law that might affect publication requirements or financial 
disclosure for committees formed primarily to support 
incorporation of a city. 
In addition, you have requested 
notification of any other rulings affecting contributions to such 
committees. 
Enclosed is the "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure 
Provisions of the Political Reform Act." It contains information 
regarding disclosure and other requirements of a committee which 
is formed primarily to support or oppose a ballot measure. 
On the 
cover of this manual is referenced the "Interim Information Manual 
on Proposition 68 and Proposition 73." 
This manual would not be 
necessary for a committee which exists exclusively to support the 
incorporation of a city. 
If you have any additional questions, please call me at (916) 
322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Enclosure 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916)322-5660 


~ 
)Jm©®~(tft@fID. C<1ru@tnJ:P) 
P.O. Box 485 
Aptos CA 95001-0485 
July 22, 1989 
Ms. Jeane Pritchard 
California Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Ste. 700 
Box 807 
Sacramento, Ca. 95807 
Dear Ms. Pritchard: 
It has come to our attention that there may have been some changes made as to 
the disclosure requirements affecting contributions made in favor of city 
incorporation efforts. 
We would greatly appreciate your sending us any information on the current 
status of publicat'ion requirements, 
disclosure laws, and rulings that 
deal with incorporation contributions. 
Sincerely Yours, 
Candace Dauphinot 
Executive Committee of the Aptos Incorporation

\ 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
July 28, 1989 
Candace 
Dauphinot 
Aptos Incorporation Group 
P.O. Box 485 
Aptos, CA 
95001-0485 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-451 
Dear Ms. Dauphinot: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on July 27, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly yours J 
f· 
• 
Jeanne Prltchard 
't 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
JP:plh 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

sections.question: You have requested information concerning any recent changes in the law that might affect publication requirements or financial disclosure for committees formed primarily to support incorporation of a city. In addition, you have requested notification of any other rulings affecting contributions to such committees.
sections.conclusion: Enclosed is the "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act." It contains information regarding disclosure and other requirements of a committee which is formed primarily to support or oppose a ballot measure. On the cover of this manual is referenced the "Interim Information Manual on Proposition 68 and Proposition 73." This manual would not be necessary for a committee which exists exclusively to support the incorporation of a city.
sections.question_synthetic: What recent changes in law affect publication requirements and financial disclosure for committees formed to support city incorporation, and what rulings govern contributions to such committees?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided the Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions, noting that committees supporting city incorporation have different requirements than committees supporting ballot measures, and that additional guidance on Propositions 68 and 73 would not apply to incorporation-only committees.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (July 22, 1989) and FPPC acknowledgment letter (July 28, 1989), followed by the substantive FPPC advice response (September 11, 1989). No formal CONCLUSION section header exists, but the substantive response paragraph serves as the conclusion. Duplicate copies of the incoming request letter are present in the document.

QUESTION: You have requested information concerning any recent changes in the law that might affect publication requirements or financial disclosure for committees formed primarily to support incorporation of a city. In addition, you have requested notification of any other rulings affecting contributions to such committees.

CONCLUSION: Enclosed is the "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act." It contains information regarding disclosure and other requirements of a committee which is formed primarily to support or oppose a ballot measure. On the cover of this manual is referenced the "Interim Information Manual on Proposition 68 and Proposition 73." This manual would not be necessary for a committee which exists exclusively to support the incorporation of a city.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to an inquiry from the Aptos Incorporation Group regarding disclosure requirements and publication rules for committees supporting city incorporation efforts. The Commission provided relevant campaign disclosure manuals and clarified that certain proposition-specific guidance was not applicable to incorporation-only committees.
